t: A belt is 14 inches long. You cut off 6 inches, then cut off 3 more inches. How long is it now?
e: 14 - 6 = 8, then 8 - 3 = 5 inches. Two steps!

t: A piano lesson starts at 3:15 PM and lasts 45 minutes. What time does it end?
e: 3:15 + 45 min = 4:00 PM. There is no such thing as 3:60!

t: A rectangle is 5 squares long and 3 squares wide. Max says the area is 8 square units. What did Max do wrong?
o: He added 5 + 3 instead of counting all squares
e: Max added 5 + 3 = 8. But the real area is 3 rows of 5 = 15 square units!

t: Mia says a giraffe is 18 inches tall. Is that reasonable?
o: No, a giraffe is about 18 feet tall
No, a giraffe is 1 inch tall
e: 18 inches is only 1.5 feet — that is the size of a small dog! A giraffe is about 18 feet tall.

t: It is 11:45 AM. What time will it be in 30 minutes?
e: 11:45 + 15 min = 12:00, then + 15 more = 12:15. It changes from AM to PM at noon! So 12:15 PM.

t: A patio is 4 by 3. You add a 2-by-3 section. What is the new total area?
e: Patio: 4 x 3 = 12. New section: 2 x 3 = 6. Total: 12 + 6 = 18 square units!

t: A rope is 2 feet 6 inches. How many inches is that in all?
e: 2 feet = 24 inches. 24 + 6 = 30 inches total!

t: Lee left home at 7:40 AM and arrived at school at 8:10 AM. How long was his trip?
e: From 7:40 to 8:00 = 20 min. From 8:00 to 8:10 = 10 min. Total = 30 minutes!

t: A rectangle has an area of 18 square units. It is 6 squares long. How many squares wide is it?
e: If 6 squares long and area is 18, then width = 18 / 6 = 3 squares wide!

t: Kai has 3 pieces of string: 5 inches, 8 inches, and 4 inches. He puts them together. How long is the total string?
e: 5 + 8 + 4 = 17 inches total!

t: Ava says the time is 5:70 PM. What should the correct time be?
e: There is no :70 on a clock! 70 minutes = 1 hour 10 min. So 5:70 should be 6:10 PM.

t: A 2-by-8 rectangle and a 4-by-4 square — which has a bigger area?
o: They are the same — both 16 square units
e: 2 x 8 = 16 and 4 x 4 = 16. They have the same area! Different shapes, same area.

t: Dan says a pencil is 7 feet long. What did he do wrong?
o: He used feet instead of inches — a pencil is about 7 inches
e: 7 feet is 84 inches — that is taller than most adults! A pencil is about 7 inches, not 7 feet.

t: A cartoon is 25 minutes long. It starts at 4:40 PM. What time does it end?
A wall is 5 by 4. You hang a picture that covers 6 square units. How many wall squares are still showing?
e: Wall: 5 x 4 = 20. Then 20 - 6 = 14 squares still showing!

t: Which is longer: 3 feet or 40 inches?
e: 3 feet = 36 inches. 40 > 36, so 40 inches is longer by 4 inches!

t: Nina says she slept for 120 minutes. How many hours is that?
e: 60 minutes = 1 hour. 120 / 60 = 2 hours!

t: A garden is 6 by 3. A flower bed covers 10 of those squares. How many garden squares are left?
e: Garden: 6 x 3 = 18. Then 18 - 10 = 8 squares left!

t: Eva has a 30-inch ribbon. She cuts it into 3 equal pieces. How long is each piece?
e: 30 / 3 = 10 inches per piece!

t: Jake played outside from 4:15 PM to 5:00 PM, then did homework from 5:00 PM to 5:30 PM. How much time in all?
e: Outside: 4:15 to 5:00 = 45 min. Homework: 5:00 to 5:30 = 30 min. Total: 45 + 30 = 75 min = 1 hour 15 minutes!
